label: LinkedIn Ads
description: Connect to LinkedIn Campaign Manager to pull ad performance, spend, and audience reporting data.

form.title: Connect LinkedIn Ads
form.subtitle: Authorize Anton to read your LinkedIn Campaign Manager ads and reporting data.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0 (Bring Your Own App)
methods.description: Connect via LinkedIn OAuth 2.0 using your own LinkedIn Developer app. Requires Marketing Developer Platform (MDP) approval from LinkedIn — a one-time application process. Once approved, paste your Client ID and Secret below and Anton handles the rest.
methods.how_to:
## LinkedIn Ads OAuth 2.0 Setup

LinkedIn Ads uses OAuth 2.0 via a Developer app you own. Access to the required marketing scopes also requires **Marketing Developer Platform (MDP) approval** from LinkedIn — apply early as it can take several days.

### Step 1 — Apply for Marketing Developer Platform access

1. Go to the [LinkedIn Marketing Developer Platform](https://business.linkedin.com/marketing-solutions/marketing-api) and click **Request access**.
2. Fill in your use case (internal analytics, reporting, etc.) and submit.
3. Wait for LinkedIn to approve your request (typically 3–7 business days). You'll receive an email confirmation.

### Step 2 — Create a LinkedIn Developer App

1. Visit [LinkedIn Developer Portal](https://www.linkedin.com/developers/apps) and click **Create app**.
2. Fill in app name, LinkedIn Page, and logo. Click **Create app**.
3. Under the **Auth** tab, add a redirect URL: `http://127.0.0.1` (or the port Anton instructs, e.g. `http://127.0.0.1:3000/callback`).
4. Note your **Client ID** and **Client Secret** from the **Auth** tab.

### Step 3 — Add required product scopes

1. On your app page, go to the **Products** tab.
2. Request access to **Marketing APIs** — this links your MDP approval to the app and unlocks `r_ads`, `r_ads_reporting`, and `rw_ads` scopes.
3. Confirm the scopes appear under **Auth → OAuth 2.0 scopes**.

### Step 4 — Connect in Anton

1. Paste your **Client ID** and **Client Secret** into the fields below.
2. Click **Submit**. Anton opens a LinkedIn consent screen in your browser.
3. Authorize the requested permissions. Anton stores the resulting tokens locally.

### Notes

- LinkedIn access tokens expire after 60 days; refresh tokens last up to 1 year. Anton will attempt silent refresh.
- If you see a scope error during authorization, confirm MDP approval is linked to the app in the Products tab.
- Tokens can be revoked at [LinkedIn App Settings](https://www.linkedin.com/developers/apps) at any time.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: Client ID
fields.description: Found under your app's Auth tab in the LinkedIn Developer Portal.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: Client Secret
fields.description: Paired with the Client ID. Keep this private — anyone with it can authorize as your app.

fields.name: ad_account_id
fields.label: Ad Account ID (optional)
fields.description: Your LinkedIn Campaign Manager account URN ID (numbers only). Leave blank to fetch all accounts accessible to the authorized user.
